turnaround_reason: Avalo is a small-cap clinical-stage biotech with lead asset AVTX-009 (anti-IL-1β mAb) in Phase 2 for inflammatory diseases. The screen captured a -94% drawdown from peak, but the basing setup is misleading — biotech moves on data, not technicals.
bullish_points: Massive net insider buying (629K shares purchased, 20 transactions) — biotech insiders rarely buy
Pristine balance sheet: $82M cash vs $328K debt; current ratio 7.7x — runway through key catalyst
Analyst mean target $46 (+217% upside) reflects strong sell-side conviction on AVTX-009 readouts
11 analysts cover for a $766M biotech — well-followed
Insiders have exercised options and sold but bought more than sold net (517 net basis points of float)
bearish_points: Pre-revenue with $-60M operating cash burn — single-asset binary biotech
AVTX-009 Phase 2 readout is binary; -50% or worse on failure
Massive recent insider option exercises and sales (CFO sold $449K, Officer sold $448K in May)
Stock 6% of 200-week MA — a -94% multi-year drawdown reflects deep history of disappointment (legacy Cerecor)
catalyst: AVTX-009 Phase 2 topline data readout — the only thing that matters
catalyst_timing: Unknown from dossier; likely H2 2026 or 2027 depending on trial progression
suggested_trade: Spot only, position-sized for total loss tolerance (binary biotech). Avoid options — too expensive at 103% IV without clear catalyst window
key_risk: AVTX-009 Phase 2 misses primary endpoint — 60%+ overnight drawdown likely
would_skip_if: Unable to confirm Phase 2 readout timeline; if insider selling accelerates; if dilutive financing announced
